primary_accounting_issue: Qualification IFRS 9 d’un actif financier au regard du critère des flux contractuels constitués uniquement de principal et d’intérêts et de ses catégories de mesure subséquente

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 9 as the single governing lineage because the question concerns classification of a financial asset based on contractual cash flow characteristics, and under the precedence rule the applicable IFRS standard governs this issue directly.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the context contains only IFRS 9 and it directly addresses classification based on solely payments of principal and interest.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern classification and subsequent measurement categories for financial assets, including the SPPI condition.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify how to assess whether contractual cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest and therefore constrain the classification outcome.
This paragraph provides a distinct alternative measurement route through designation at fair value through profit or loss.

peripheral_authority.reason: These agenda decisions address narrower fact patterns and are not needed to identify the peer top-level classification approaches for this issue.

treatment_families.family: Subsequent measurement of financial assets when the instrument has solely payments of principal and interest and is assessed together with the business model
Optional designation of a financial asset at fair value through profit or loss to eliminate or significantly reduce an accounting mismatch

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies amortised cost as a top-level subsequent measurement category when the business model is to hold to collect contractual cash flows and the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through other comprehensive income as a top-level subsequent measurement category when the business model is achieved by both collecting contractual cash flows and selling financial assets, provided the cash flows are solely payments of principal and interest.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value through profit or loss as the residual top-level measurement category and also permits designation into this category to address an accounting mismatch.
